artifacts.summary: Official University of Potsdam sources include a presidency-adopted AI teaching position paper, a 2026 AI-and-examinations handbook, an e-assessment AI page, GPT.UP service information, and the ZfQ AI-in-teaching resource page. Workshop slides and repository research papers were treated as limited or rejected for policy claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam
Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.claimText: Universität Potsdam's adopted AI teaching position paper supports the use of generative AI in teaching so students can develop reflective use for future professional practice.

citation.citationTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.claimText: The position paper says students and teaching staff cannot be obliged or asked to use ChatGPT unless technical and organizational measures prevent OpenAI from processing their personal data, and prompts must not contain personal or confidential university information.

citation.citationTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.claimText: The 2026 AI-and-examinations handbook advises examiners to state early and in writing whether and how AI may be used in a course and assessment, and to use transparency rules when AI is allowed or cannot be explicitly excluded.

citation.citationTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.claimText: The 2026 AI-and-examinations handbook states that AI detectors are not reliable and do not provide legally secure proof of deception.

citation.citationTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.claimText: The official e-assessment AI page says using AI such as GPT.UP may not be a condition for passing an assessment, and AI should not be used by instructors for full creation and grading of summative examinations.
The official GPT.UP page says GPT.UP has been available to all members of the University of Potsdam since 14 October 2024, with login through the UP account.

The ZfQ AI-in-teaching page says GPT.UP is available to University of Potsdam members and that its use ensures AI providers do not process students' personal data and student data is processed only for university-defined teaching purposes.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Grundsätzlich befürwortet die Universität Potsdam den Einsatz auch von generativer KI in der Lehre, um einen reflektierten Einsatz für die zukünftige Berufspraxis der Studierenden zu gewährleisten.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The University of Potsdam supports generative AI in teaching to support reflective use for students' future professional practice.

citation.citationTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.sourceTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Dies bedeutet, dass Studierende und Lehrende nicht verpflichtet oder gebeten werden können, ChatGPT einzusetzen, es sei denn, es kann durch technische und organisatorische Maßnahmen sichergestellt werden, dass ihre personenbezogenen Daten bei der Nutzung durch OpenAI nicht verarbeitet werden können.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students and instructors cannot be required or asked to use ChatGPT unless safeguards ensure OpenAI cannot process their personal data.

citation.citationTitle: Positionspapier KI in der Hochschullehre an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.sourceTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Es ist daher notwendig, frühzeitig (idealerweise zu Semesterbeginn) und schriftlich zu präzisieren, ob und in welcher Form KI in der Lehrveranstaltung und in der Prüfung verwendet werden darf.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Examiners should specify early and in writing whether and how AI may be used in the course and assessment.

citation.citationTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.sourceTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: KI-Detektoren sind nicht zuverlässig und liefern keine rechtssicheren Beweise, da sie lediglich die statistische Wahrscheinlichkeit bewerten.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI detectors are not reliable and do not provide legally secure proof because they assess only statistical probability.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 1 Rechtlicher Rahmen und die Rolle der Prüfenden

citation.citationTitle: Handreichung KI und Prüfungen an der Universität Potsdam

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Der Einsatz von KI (wie z.B. GPT.UP) darf keine Bedingung zum Bestehen einer Prüfungsleistung sein. KI (wie z.B. GPT.UP) sollte von Lehrenden nicht zur vollumfänglichen Erstellung und Bewertung summativer Prüfungen verwendet werden.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Using AI such as GPT.UP may not be a condition for passing an assessment, and instructors should not use it for full creation and grading of summative exams.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Grundsätzlich sind folgende Aspekte im Kontext von KI und e-Assessment zu beachten

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: GPT.UP wurde am Montag, den 14.10.2024, freigeschaltet und steht seitdem allen Angehörigen der UP zur Verfügung. Der Login erfolgt über den UP-Account.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: GPT.UP has been available to all UP members since 14 October 2024, with login through the UP account.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: GPT.UP steht allen Angehörigen der Universität Potsdam zur Verfügung. Die Verwendung stellt sicher, dass die KI-Anbieter keine personenbezogenen Daten der Studierenden (Login-Daten, Metadaten) verarbeiten.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: GPT.UP is available to University of Potsdam members and is presented as preventing AI providers from processing students' personal data.

artifacts.notes: Narrow claim matches the official position paper wording and does not convert guidance into a broader mandate.
Claim preserves the source's conditional privacy phrasing and does not overstate a complete ban.
Claim uses advised/should-style language consistent with the handbook and avoids turning guidance into a binding rule.
Claim directly follows the handbook's statement about AI-detector reliability and legal proof.
Claim combines two adjacent e-assessment bullets and preserves the source's may-not/should-not distinction.
Claim is scoped to public service availability and does not crawl login-only GPT.UP functionality.
Claim is limited to the ZfQ page's stated teaching-data handling representation for GPT.UP.

artifacts.title: Universität Potsdam AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found official University of Potsdam AI teaching and assessment guidance: a presidency-adopted position paper, a 2026 AI-and-examinations handbook, an e-assessment AI page, GPT.UP service information, and a ZfQ AI teaching page. Claims are limited to teaching support, privacy constraints around commercial AI, assessment communication, detector reliability, e-assessment restrictions, and GPT.UP availability/privacy statements.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Workshop slides and research repository papers were not used as institutional policy evidence.
The GPT.UP application itself is login-gated; only the public official service page was used.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
